document.title: Anaemia in Pregnancy — UK Clinical Guideline: clinical logic
document.status: DRAFT — not for clinical use until sign-off

evidence_tiers.established_uk_standard: Written into a current UK national guideline (BSH, NICE, RCOG) or statutory (MHRA, SPC, NHS SCT).
evidence_tiers.specialist_consensus: Opinion or pragmatic practice in a UK guideline without an RCT.
evidence_tiers.emerging_optimisation: New evidence that may revise practice; not yet UK standard.

iron_status_rules.ferritin_interpretation: Serum ferritin <30 µg/L confirms iron deficiency in pregnancy.

ferritin_masked_by_inflammation.trigger: Normal/raised ferritin with microcytic indices, TSAT <20%, or raised CRP
ferritin_masked_by_inflammation.action: Do not use ferritin alone to rule out iron deficiency; review in context.

emerging_practice.label: HOW Collaborative 2025 consensus
emerging_practice.proposal: Routine ferritin at booking and 24–28 weeks.
emerging_practice.uk_baseline: Not current BSH or NICE policy.

diagnostic_pathway.action: Offer FBC at booking and 28 weeks.

no.action: Routine care; ferritin only if risk factors.

diagnostic_pathway.action: Send serum ferritin ± transferrin saturation, CRP.

yes.action: Diagnose iron deficiency anaemia; enter treatment pathway.

no.action: Ferritin equivocal — check TSAT and CRP.

diagnostic_pathway.action: If iron deficiency excluded, consider B12/folate deficiency, haemoglobinopathy (do not start empirical iron), AIHA, microangiopathy, anaemia of chronic disease.
Trial of oral iron as a diagnostic test acceptable when anaemia mild and indices suggestive; Hb rise ~10 g/L in 2 weeks supports iron deficiency.

iron_deficiency_correction.action: FBC + ferritin; correct deficiency before conception where feasible

haemoglobinopathy_screening.action: Offer per NHS Sickle Cell and Thalassaemia Screening Programme where status unknown

counselling: Take on an empty stomach 30–60 minutes before a meal
Avoid simultaneous tea, coffee, calcium, antacids
Vitamin C (orange juice or 250 mg ascorbic acid) improves absorption
Warn about nausea, constipation, black stools
Offer stool softener or laxative if constipated

emerging_optimisation.label: Low-dose / alternate-day oral iron
emerging_optimisation.basis: Moretti physiological studies; Churchill 2025 UK cohort reports 36.5% achieved 10 g/L rise on ferrous sulfate 200 mg TDS
emerging_optimisation.status: Not the UK baseline for established IDA until national guidance changes

iv_iron.trimester_window: Generally confine to 2nd and 3rd trimesters unless clearly necessary

indications.criterion: Oral iron intolerance despite different formulation and dose
True non-response to adequate oral iron after 2 weeks with confirmed adherence
Severe anaemia (Hb <90 g/L) from second trimester
Late presentation >=34 weeks with iron deficiency anaemia and insufficient time for oral response
Preference for fewer doses in selected women

iv_iron.dose_calculation: Body-weight and Hb-based method per current SPC; observe per-session and per-week maxima; check medicines.org.uk before prescribing.

consent_requirements: Ferric carboxymaltose: risk of symptomatic hypophosphataemia

phosphate_monitoring_after_fcm_if_any: Repeated dosing or high cumulative dose

third_trimester_timing.label: Pragmatic expert practice
third_trimester_timing.note: Not a sourced national recommendation; attending teams decide based on gestation, severity, and logistics.

rapidiron_2025_derman.applicability: Baseline Hb more severe than typical UK IDA; supportive not decisive.

when_to_test: Clinical features of B12 deficiency (neurological, glossitis)

options: Oral cyanocobalamin at least 1 mg daily (if dietary cause likely and absorption intact)
IM hydroxocobalamin 1 mg three times weekly for 2 weeks, then 1 mg every 2–3 months

b12_with_neurology.if_advice_unavailable: IM hydroxocobalamin 1 mg alternate days until no further improvement, then 1 mg every 2 months

folate_replacement.dose: Folic acid 5 mg orally once daily until deficiency resolves
folate_replacement.then_continue: 400 µg daily (or 5 mg daily in higher-risk groups) for remainder of pregnancy

do_not_give_folate_alone.reason: Risk of subacute combined degeneration of the spinal cord if B12 also deficient

haemoglobinopathy_cross_reference.sickle_cell_disease: BSH 2021 (Oteng-Ntim et al.); supersedes RCOG GTG 61
haemoglobinopathy_cross_reference.thalassaemia: BSH 2024 (Shah et al.); supersedes RCOG GTG 66
haemoglobinopathy_cross_reference.screening_lab_diagnosis: BSH 2023 (Bain et al.); NHS SCT Programme

iron_rules.transfusion_dependent_thalassaemia_preco: Switch deferasirox/deferiprone to desferrioxamine 6–12 weeks before planned conception

escalation_triggers: Hb fall >20 g/L in 24 h without overt bleeding
Suspected TTP (send ADAMTS13, prepare plasma exchange)

check_hb_within_48h_if: Symptoms: tachycardia, breathlessness, dizziness, severe fatigue

caesarean_section_alone.note: Record as local policy if Trust routinely checks Hb after caesarean section

stratified_treatment.action: Oral iron; reassurance; review at 6-week postnatal check
Red cell transfusion (typically single unit with reassessment) + IV iron to replete stores

cmv_seronegative.elective_pregnancy_transfusion: Where indicated and feasible without delay

product_selection.emergency_group: Group O RhD-negative, K-negative (only if emergency release required)

audit_indicators.indicator: Women with FBC at booking and 28 weeks

audit_indicators.target: Benchmark locally; improve year on year

audit_indicators.indicator: Serious adverse events following IV iron
